listenings.title: Nice to Meet You
listenings.description: Ana and Tom meet for the first time. Practice greetings and the verbs like, speak, drink and eat.

dialogue.speaker: Tom
dialogue.line: Good morning! I'm Tom.

dialogue.speaker: Ana
dialogue.line: Hello, Tom! I'm Ana. Nice to meet you!

dialogue.speaker: Tom
dialogue.line: Nice to meet you too, Ana. Do you speak English?

dialogue.speaker: Ana
dialogue.line: Yes, I speak English. I speak Portuguese too. Do you speak Portuguese?

dialogue.speaker: Tom
dialogue.line: No, I speak English. I like your language!

dialogue.speaker: Ana
dialogue.line: Thank you! Do you like coffee?

dialogue.speaker: Tom
dialogue.line: Yes! I like coffee. I drink coffee every morning.

dialogue.speaker: Ana
dialogue.line: I like tea. I don't drink coffee.

dialogue.speaker: Tom
dialogue.line: Do you like to eat in the morning?

dialogue.speaker: Ana
dialogue.line: Yes, I eat breakfast with my brother. You?

dialogue.speaker: Tom
dialogue.line: I eat with my father. He likes coffee too!

dialogue.speaker: Ana
dialogue.line: Ha! Good morning to you and your father, Tom!

questions.question: What languages does Ana speak?
What does Tom drink every morning?
What does Ana prefer to drink?
Who does Ana eat breakfast with?
Who also likes coffee?

listenings.title: First Day at the Office
listenings.description: Tom starts his first day at work. Practice work vocabulary, needs, and polite expressions.

dialogue.speaker: Ana
dialogue.line: Good morning, Tom! Welcome to the office.

dialogue.speaker: Tom
dialogue.line: Thank you, Ana! I'm happy to be here.

dialogue.speaker: Ana
dialogue.line: Do you work with my father?

dialogue.speaker: Tom
dialogue.line: Yes, I work with your father. And I work with you now!

dialogue.speaker: Ana
dialogue.line: Great! Please, sit down. Do you need anything?

dialogue.speaker: Tom
dialogue.line: No, thank you. I don't need anything right now.

dialogue.speaker: Ana
dialogue.line: I need to study the new project today. Do you?

dialogue.speaker: Tom
dialogue.line: Yes, I need to study too. And I need to work very hard.

dialogue.speaker: Ana
dialogue.line: Sorry, I need to go now. I work with your brother today.

dialogue.speaker: Tom
dialogue.line: No problem! Does your brother work here too?

dialogue.speaker: Ana
dialogue.line: Yes! He doesn't need to study — he likes to work!

dialogue.speaker: Tom
dialogue.line: Ha! Good-bye, Ana. See you later.

dialogue.speaker: Ana
dialogue.line: Bye-bye, Tom! Good luck on your first day!

questions.question: Who does Tom work with at the office?
Does Tom need anything when he arrives?
What does Ana need to do today?
Why does Ana leave?
What does Ana's brother like to do?

listenings.title: Do You Want to Go?
listenings.description: Ana invites Tom to the movies. Practice want, go, sleep, have and everyday expressions.

dialogue.speaker: Tom
dialogue.line: Hi, Ana! How are you?

dialogue.speaker: Ana
dialogue.line: I'm fine, Tom! And you?

dialogue.speaker: Tom
dialogue.line: Not bad. I want to go to the movies today. Do you want to go?

dialogue.speaker: Ana
dialogue.line: Yes! I want to go. Do you have a boss today?

dialogue.speaker: Tom
dialogue.line: No, I don't have to work today. I only work on the weekends.

dialogue.speaker: Ana
dialogue.line: My brother wants to go too. He doesn't sleep all day today!

dialogue.speaker: Tom
dialogue.line: Ha! Does he have to go to school?

dialogue.speaker: Ana
dialogue.line: No, he doesn't have to go to school today. He wants to go with us.

dialogue.speaker: Tom
dialogue.line: Good! Do you want to go downtown or to the store first?

dialogue.speaker: Ana
dialogue.line: Let's go to the store. I need to buy something.

dialogue.speaker: Tom
dialogue.line: OK! I don't want to go alone. I go with you and your brother!

dialogue.speaker: Ana
dialogue.line: Great! See you there, Tom. Let's go!

questions.question: How is Ana feeling?
Where does Tom want to go?
Does Tom have to work today?
When does Tom work?
Who else wants to go to the movies?

listenings.title: I Only Like English
listenings.description: Tom and Ana talk about studying languages. A review of lessons 1–3 using do/does questions.

dialogue.speaker: Tom
dialogue.line: Ana, do you like to study English?

dialogue.speaker: Ana
dialogue.line: Yes! I like English a lot. I study at home with my parents.

dialogue.speaker: Tom
dialogue.line: Do your parents speak English and French?

dialogue.speaker: Ana
dialogue.line: Yes, they speak English and French. But I don't like French.

dialogue.speaker: Tom
dialogue.line: Do you only like English?

dialogue.speaker: Ana
dialogue.line: Yes! I only like English. I want to speak English very well.

dialogue.speaker: Tom
dialogue.line: Do you study English every day?

dialogue.speaker: Ana
dialogue.line: I only study on the weekends. But I want to study every day.

dialogue.speaker: Tom
dialogue.line: Does your brother study English too?

dialogue.speaker: Ana
dialogue.line: No, he doesn't. He doesn't like to study. He only likes to work!

dialogue.speaker: Tom
dialogue.line: Ha! And your parents — do they work downtown?

dialogue.speaker: Ana
dialogue.line: Yes, they work downtown every day. They don't work at home.

questions.question: Where does Ana study English?
What languages do Ana's parents speak?
Does Ana like French?
When does Ana study English?
What does Ana's brother like to do?

listenings.title: Do You Know How to Sell?
listenings.description: Tom wants to buy a car. Ana's father sells cars. Practice understand, buy, sell and know how to.

dialogue.speaker: Tom
dialogue.line: Ana, I want to buy a car. Do you know a good place?

dialogue.speaker: Ana
dialogue.line: Yes! My father sells cars. He has a big store downtown.

dialogue.speaker: Tom
dialogue.line: Nice! Does he sell small cars too?

dialogue.speaker: Ana
dialogue.line: Yes, he sells big cars and small cars. New and old.

dialogue.speaker: Tom
dialogue.line: I need to buy a small car for my wife. She doesn't know how to drive a big car.

dialogue.speaker: Ana
dialogue.line: My father understands. He can help you. Do you understand Portuguese well?

dialogue.speaker: Tom
dialogue.line: I understand a little Portuguese. But I don't know how to speak very well.

dialogue.speaker: Ana
dialogue.line: That's OK! My father speaks English. He knows how to speak a lot.

dialogue.speaker: Tom
dialogue.line: How much money do I need to buy a small car?

dialogue.speaker: Ana
dialogue.line: I don't know exactly. You need to speak with my father at work.

dialogue.speaker: Tom
dialogue.line: How many cars does he have at the store?

dialogue.speaker: Ana
dialogue.line: He has a lot of cars! Go to the store today. He wants to sell!

questions.question: What does Tom want to buy?
Where is Ana's father's store?
Why does Tom need a small car?
options: His wife can't drive big cars

questions.question: Does Ana's father speak English?
options: Yes, he knows how to speak

questions.question: When should Tom go to the store?

listenings.title: Let's Study Together
listenings.description: Mike wants to learn English. Joanne invites him to study at home. Review of lessons 1–5.

dialogue.speaker: Mike
dialogue.line: Hi, Joanne! Good morning! How are you?

dialogue.speaker: Joanne
dialogue.line: Fine, Mike! And you?

dialogue.speaker: Mike
dialogue.line: Not bad. I need to speak English, but I don't know how.

dialogue.speaker: Joanne
dialogue.line: Do you want to learn English? I study at home with my parents.

dialogue.speaker: Mike
dialogue.line: Yes! I need to speak English a lot. Do you know how to speak very well?

dialogue.speaker: Joanne
dialogue.line: Yes, I know how to speak. My parents help me every day.

dialogue.speaker: Mike
dialogue.line: I don't understand English very well. And I don't have a teacher.

dialogue.speaker: Joanne
dialogue.line: That's OK! Tomorrow come to my house and we can study together.

dialogue.speaker: Mike
dialogue.line: Do your parents work at home?

dialogue.speaker: Joanne
dialogue.line: My father works downtown and my mother works at a school. But they study with me at night.

dialogue.speaker: Mike
dialogue.line: Very good! See you there tomorrow. Bye!

dialogue.speaker: Joanne
dialogue.line: Bye, Mike! See you tomorrow morning!

questions.question: What does Mike need?
Where does Joanne study English?
When does Joanne invite Mike to come?
Where does Joanne's father work?
When do Joanne's parents study with her?

listenings.title: I Prefer to Read
listenings.description: Tom and Ana talk about books, letters and writing. Practice prefer, help, write and read.

dialogue.speaker: Tom
dialogue.line: Ana, do you like to read?

dialogue.speaker: Ana
dialogue.line: Yes! I prefer to read books. I read every day.

dialogue.speaker: Tom
dialogue.line: Do you read magazines too?

dialogue.speaker: Ana
dialogue.line: Yes, I read magazines and newspapers. My father reads a lot.

dialogue.speaker: Tom
dialogue.line: Do you write letters?

dialogue.speaker: Ana
dialogue.line: Yes, I write to my mother every week. This week I want to write about my new apartment.

dialogue.speaker: Tom
dialogue.line: Do you have a new apartment downtown?

dialogue.speaker: Ana
dialogue.line: Yes! My new apartment is big. I have a lot of books there.

dialogue.speaker: Tom
dialogue.line: How many books do you have?

dialogue.speaker: Ana
dialogue.line: About twenty books. I need to buy more! Do you want to go to the store with me tomorrow morning?

dialogue.speaker: Tom
dialogue.line: Yes! I want to help you buy books. Tonight I need to write to my mother too.

dialogue.speaker: Ana
dialogue.line: Good! Bye Tom. See you tomorrow morning!

questions.question: What does Ana prefer to do?
Who reads a lot in Ana's family?
What does Ana write about this week?
About how many books does Ana have?
What does Tom need to do tonight?

listenings.title: On the Farm
listenings.description: Tom and Ana talk about Ana's family farm. Review of lessons 1–7 with new words: always, because.

dialogue.speaker: Tom
dialogue.line: Ana, do you have a farm?

dialogue.speaker: Ana
dialogue.line: Yes! My parents have a big farm. I always go there on the weekends.

dialogue.speaker: Tom
dialogue.line: Do you like the farm?

dialogue.speaker: Ana
dialogue.line: Yes! I prefer to go to the farm because I don't have to study there.

dialogue.speaker: Tom
dialogue.line: Ha! What do you do there?

dialogue.speaker: Ana
dialogue.line: I always help my father. And I play with my brother and my sister a lot.

dialogue.speaker: Tom
dialogue.line: Do you write to your mother when you are at the farm?

dialogue.speaker: Ana
dialogue.line: Yes, I want to write a letter to my mother because I don't want to go home tomorrow.

dialogue.speaker: Tom
dialogue.line: Can you write very well?

dialogue.speaker: Ana
dialogue.line: I write with my sister because I don't know how to write very well alone.

dialogue.speaker: Tom
dialogue.line: Do you read at the farm?

dialogue.speaker: Ana
dialogue.line: Yes, I only know how to read. I prefer to read, not write! Bye Tom!

questions.question: When does Ana always go to the farm?
Why does Ana prefer the farm?
Who does Ana play with at the farm?
Who helps Ana write letters?
What does Ana prefer to do — read or write?

listenings.title: Where Do You Live?
listenings.description: Tom asks Ana about her home and daily routine. Practice live, cook, open, close and daily meal expressions.

dialogue.speaker: Tom
dialogue.line: Ana, where do you live?

dialogue.speaker: Ana
dialogue.line: I live downtown with my parents. We have a big apartment here.

dialogue.speaker: Tom
dialogue.line: Do you cook at home?

dialogue.speaker: Ana
dialogue.line: Yes! My mother usually cooks for us. She cooks rice and beans every day.

dialogue.speaker: Tom
dialogue.line: Do you have lunch at home?

dialogue.speaker: Ana
dialogue.line: Yes, we usually have lunch together. I like to eat salad too.

dialogue.speaker: Tom
dialogue.line: What time do you get up every day?

dialogue.speaker: Ana
dialogue.line: I usually get up early because I have to go to work.

dialogue.speaker: Tom
dialogue.line: Do you open the store every day?

dialogue.speaker: Ana
dialogue.line: Yes, I open the store in the morning and I close it at night.

dialogue.speaker: Tom
dialogue.line: Do your neighbors live downtown too?

dialogue.speaker: Ana
dialogue.line: Some neighbors live here. But my friend lives in the country. She prefers the country!

questions.question: Where does Ana live?
Who usually cooks at Ana's home?
What does Ana's mother cook every day?
Why does Ana get up early?
options: She has to go to work

questions.question: Where does Ana's friend prefer to live?

listenings.title: Life in the Country
listenings.description: Ana's friend Rosa talks about living in the country. Final review of all Book One vocabulary and structures.

dialogue.speaker: Tom
dialogue.line: Ana, who is that girl?

dialogue.speaker: Ana
dialogue.line: That is my friend Rosa. She lives in the country with her parents.

dialogue.speaker: Tom
dialogue.line: Does she like to live there?

dialogue.speaker: Ana
dialogue.line: Yes! She prefers to live in the country because she has many friends there. They are her neighbors.

dialogue.speaker: Tom
dialogue.line: Does she work?

dialogue.speaker: Ana
dialogue.line: She studies at school. Her friends help her with her homework every day.

dialogue.speaker: Tom
dialogue.line: What does she usually eat?

dialogue.speaker: Ana
dialogue.line: She usually has lunch at home. She likes to eat rice, beans, and salad.

dialogue.speaker: Tom
dialogue.line: Does she cook?

dialogue.speaker: Ana
dialogue.line: Yes! She cooks for her parents. She knows how to cook very well.

dialogue.speaker: Tom
dialogue.line: Does she want to go to the city?

dialogue.speaker: Ana
dialogue.line: Yes, she has to buy a ticket to go to the city today. She needs to buy clothes for school.

questions.question: Who is Rosa?
Why does Rosa prefer the country?
Who helps Rosa with her homework?
What does Rosa usually eat for lunch?
Why does Rosa need to go to the city today?
